summary: In this historical reenactment interview, Andrew Carnegie recounts his early life, from his impoverished childhood in Scotland to his family's immigration to Pennsylvania. He describes his early jobs as a bobbin boy and telegraph operator, culminating in his first investment and his realization of the power of earning money through capital rather than labor.

signature_quotes.text: we were astounded to receive the first check for monies that I did not have to earn
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on the dividend returns from his very first capital investment in the O'Reilly Telegraph company.
